title: hieracium krivanense
side_effects: Hieracium krivanense can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and vomiting, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged exposure to these compounds can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation and respiratory problems. In severe cases, improper use of Hieracium krivanense may result in cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure. If ingested in large quantities, the plant may cause kidney damage and other systemic problems.
